metadata.title: 10th Pass Production Readiness: 12 Fixes from 14-Agent Deep Exploration
metadata.approach: TDD: RED → GREEN → VERIFY → FIX → CHECKPOINT → CONVERGE
metadata.phasing: Phase 1 (P0 blockers) → Phase 2 (P1 resilience) → Phase 3 (P1 observability+consistency)
metadata.verification: make test-staging (must pass 6558+ tests, 0 skips, 0 failures)

context.summary:
The 10th debug factorial pass (18 glitch-only cells targeting SAM3 TopoLoRA, SAM3 Hybrid, and zero-shot baselines) discovered 7 bugs and 127+ test gaps via 14 reviewer agents across 4 waves. 88 new tests were written, 3 bugs fixed, but 3 critical blockers remain for production launch.
This plan addresses the top 12 production-readiness fixes in priority order. Each task follows TDD: write failing test FIRST, then implement.
Source report: docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-10th-pass-report.md

prior-pass-root-causes.root-cause: NaN loss at init for cbdice_cldice — SoftclDiceLoss degenerates with random weights on 98% background data
MLflow resume has no retry — unreachable server causes silent fresh-start (50 epochs lost)
Training logs not observable — sky jobs logs is slow/blocking/unstructured for LLM monitoring
Docker image GAR path duplicated in 9+ files with zero consistency tests
GCS bucket names duplicated in 13+ files with partial tests only
Empty folds_to_run silently skips all training (Rule 25 violation)
Empty dataloader dict silently returns (Rule 25 violation)
load_checkpoint returns random weights on state_dict mismatch (silent corruption)
LogisticRegression crashes on all-0 labels in calibration metrics
SkeletonRecallLoss silently changes semantics when skeleton degenerates
Setup script YAMLs inconsistently hardened (only factorial has retries)
POST_TRAINING_METHODS has legacy two-var fallback (Rule 26 violation)

constraints.constraint: CLAUDE.md Rule 3: Library-first — use MONAI/PyTorch, never rewrite
CLAUDE.md Rule 16: No regex for structured data — use ast/yaml/json parsers
CLAUDE.md Rule 25: Loud failures, never silent discards
CLAUDE.md Rule 26: Greenfield — delete old conventions, no backward compat
CLAUDE.md Rule 29: Zero hardcoded parameters — read from config
CLAUDE.md Rule 31: Zero improvisation on declarative configs
Do NOT modify SkyPilot YAML env vars or accelerators without explicit instruction
All tests must pass: make test-staging (6558+ passed, 0 skipped, 0 failed)

context.cold-start-instructions:
To execute this plan from a fresh session with zero context:
1. Read THIS file completely 2. Read the source report: docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-10th-pass-report.md 3. Read CLAUDE.md (project rules) 4. Invoke /self-learning-iterative-coder 5. The skill will execute tasks in order, TDD style
Key context: The 10th debug factorial pass found that cbdice_cldice loss produces NaN at model initialization due to degenerate soft skeleton computation in MONAI's SoftclDiceLoss. This blocks 25% of factorial cells. The MLflow resume check has no retry, meaning spot preemption + MLflow outage = training restarts from epoch 0. Training logs are unstructured text, making 60+ job monitoring infeasible.

phase.name: P0 Blockers
phase.description: Fix the 3 issues that block any production factorial launch

task.title: Fix NaN loss at init for cbdice_cldice
task.description:
SoftclDiceLoss produces NaN when soft skeleton degenerates with random model weights on 98% background vessel data. Root cause: 10 iterations of morphological erosion on uniform ~0.5 probabilities → near-zero skeleton → FP32 rounding in cl_dice = 1.0 - 2*(tprec*tsens)/(tprec+tsens).
Fix: Add NaN guard in VesselCompoundLoss.forward() that clamps the unstable division, AND add a degenerate-input test for the loss_sanity pre-training check.
IMPORTANT: SoftclDiceLoss is from MONAI (imported at loss_functions.py:18 as `from monai.losses.cldice import SoftclDiceLoss`). Do NOT modify MONAI code. The fix must be in OUR wrapper (VesselCompoundLoss or CbDiceClDiceLoss).

tdd-spec.red:
Add test_cbdice_cldice_nan_at_init_all_background() to test_loss_nan_check.py: - Create random logits (torch.randn(1, 2, 8, 16, 16)) - Create ALL-BACKGROUND labels (torch.zeros(1, 1, 8, 16, 16)) - Build cbdice_cldice loss via build_loss_function("cbdice_cldice") - Assert loss is finite (not NaN, not Inf) - This test WILL FAIL with current code (NaN from SoftclDiceLoss)
Also add test_cbdice_cldice_gradient_at_init_all_background(): - Same setup but verify backward() produces finite gradients
tdd-spec.green:
In VesselCompoundLoss.forward() (loss_functions.py:106-123): After line 118: `cldice_loss = self.cldice(probs, labels_onehot)` Insert NaN guard at line 119 (before the weighted sum at line 120): if torch.isnan(cldice_loss) or torch.isinf(cldice_loss): logger.warning("clDice produced %s — falling back to 0.0", cldice_loss.item()) cldice_loss = torch.tensor(0.0, device=logits.device, requires_grad=True)
This is the MINIMAL fix. The clDice component returns 0 on degenerate input (which is semantically correct — there is no centerline to match). The DiceCE component still provides gradient signal.
tdd-spec.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_loss_nan_check.py -v make test-staging

acceptance-criteria.criterion: cbdice_cldice loss is finite with all-background labels
cbdice_cldice gradient is finite with all-background labels
All existing loss tests still pass
NaN guard logs a warning (not silent)

task.title: Add MLflow resume retry with checkpoint fallback
task.description:
check_resume_state_task() (train_flow.py:288-328) catches ALL exceptions from mlflow.get_run() and returns None (treating run as stale). If MLflow Cloud Run is temporarily unreachable during spot recovery, training silently restarts from epoch 0 instead of resuming from the checkpoint.
Fix: Add retry logic (3 attempts, 10s backoff) for MLflow get_run(). If ALL retries fail, fall back to checkpoint-file-based resume (if epoch_latest.pth exists, assume the run is valid and resume).

tdd-spec.red:
Add to test_spot_resume.py: test_resume_mlflow_unreachable_with_checkpoint(): - Mock mlflow.get_run to raise ConnectionError on all 3 attempts - Create a valid epoch_latest.yaml + epoch_latest.pth in checkpoint_dir - Call check_resume_state_task() - Assert it returns the resume state (not None) — checkpoint-based fallback - Assert a warning is logged about MLflow unreachability
test_resume_mlflow_unreachable_no_checkpoint(): - Mock mlflow.get_run to raise ConnectionError - Do NOT create epoch_latest.pth - Call check_resume_state_task() - Assert returns None (no checkpoint to fall back on)

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Resume works when MLflow is unreachable but checkpoint exists
Fresh start when MLflow unreachable AND no checkpoint
3 retry attempts with backoff before fallback

task.title: Guard empty folds_to_run and empty dataloaders
task.description: train_flow.py:1462 — if folds_to_run=[] (e.g., num_folds=0 or splits[:0]), the training loop at line 855 silently skips ALL folds. No error, no warning. train_flow.py:546-556 — if build_train_loader/build_val_loader return loaders with zero samples (empty data dirs), training proceeds with no data. Both violate Rule 25 (loud failures).

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_train_flow_empty_guards.py:
test_empty_folds_to_run_raises(): - Read train_flow.py source with AST - Find the folds_to_run assignment and the for-loop - Verify there is a guard: `if not folds_to_run: raise ValueError(...)` - OR: mock training_subflow and call with num_folds=0 - Assert ValueError is raised with message containing "folds_to_run"
test_empty_train_dicts_raises(): - Mock build_train_loader to be called with train_dicts=[] - Verify there is a guard at train_flow.py:546 that checks len(train_dicts) > 0 - Assert ValueError is raised with message about empty training data

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Empty folds_to_run raises ValueError (not silent skip)
Empty dataloaders raises ValueError (not silent return)

phase.name: P1 Resilience
phase.description: Fix silent failures that waste cloud credits at production scale

task.title: Guard load_checkpoint against state_dict mismatch
task.description: builder.py:474-512 — load_checkpoint() has partial error handling (lines 499-509 catch RuntimeError and try inner_net fallback). But if 45% of keys are missing, load_state_dict(strict=False) silently ignores them, producing a corrupted model. Add a pre-check for >50% missing keys BEFORE the existing try/except at line 498. The existing fallback logic (inner_net at line 500) should remain for legitimate DDP/wrapper key prefix mismatches.

tdd-spec.red:
test_load_checkpoint_mismatched_keys_raises(): - Create a checkpoint with keys {"conv1.weight", "conv1.bias"} - Create a model expecting {"encoder.weight", "encoder.bias"} - Call load_checkpoint(model, checkpoint_path) - Assert it raises RuntimeError or logs error (not silently returns)
test_load_checkpoint_valid_keys_succeeds(): - Create checkpoint matching model state_dict exactly - Assert load succeeds and model weights are updated
tdd-spec.green: In builder.py load_checkpoint(): After loading state_dict, check key overlap: missing = set(model.state_dict().keys()) - set(loaded_state.keys()) unexpected = set(loaded_state.keys()) - set(model.state_dict().keys()) if len(missing) > len(model.state_dict()) * 0.5: raise RuntimeError( f"Checkpoint has >50% missing keys ({len(missing)}/{len(model.state_dict())}). " f"This likely means a model architecture mismatch. " f"Missing: {list(missing)[:5]}..." )

task.title: Fix calibration metrics crash on all-zero labels
task.description: calibration_metrics.py:82 — compute_calibration_metrics() calls LogisticRegression.fit(X, y) where y can be all-zeros (all-background batch). sklearn raises ValueError on single-class targets. This crashes the analysis flow instead of returning a graceful "not applicable" result.

tdd-spec.red:
test_calibration_all_zero_labels_no_crash(): - probs = np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.99, size=1000) - labels = np.zeros(1000, dtype=np.int32) # ALL background - result = compute_calibration_metrics(probs, labels) - Assert result is a dict (not crash), with NaN or "N/A" for IPA/slope
test_calibration_all_one_labels_no_crash(): - Same but labels = np.ones(1000)
test_calibration_normal_case_unchanged(): - Mixed labels, verify existing behavior preserved
tdd-spec.green: In compute_calibration_metrics(): Before LogisticRegression.fit(), check class count: unique_labels = np.unique(y_binary) if len(unique_labels) < 2: logger.warning("Calibration: single-class labels (all %d) — LR not applicable", unique_labels[0]) ipa = float("nan") cal_slope = float("nan") else: # existing LR code

task.title: Fix SkeletonRecallLoss silent semantics change
task.description: skeleton_recall.py:100 — when skeletonize() returns empty (structure too thin), the fallback uses the FULL foreground mask as "skeleton". This silently changes the loss from skeleton-recall to plain-recall without any warning. Must log a warning when falling back.

tdd-spec.red:
test_skeleton_recall_thin_structure_warns(): - Create a 1-voxel-wide line in 3D (minimal structure) - Compute SkeletonRecallLoss - Assert a WARNING is logged about empty skeleton fallback - Assert loss is still finite (graceful degradation, but LOUD)
test_skeleton_recall_normal_structure_no_warning(): - Create a thick tube structure (skeletonizable) - Assert no warning logged
tdd-spec.green: In skeleton_recall.py _skeletonize_gt() (around line 100): After the `if skel_np.sum() == 0:` branch: logger.warning( "SkeletonRecallLoss: skeleton is empty after skeletonize() — " "falling back to full foreground mask. This changes loss semantics " "from skeleton-recall to plain-recall. Structure may be too thin." )

task.title: Fix _average_checkpoints with mismatched state_dict keys
task.description: post_training_flow.py:669 — _average_checkpoints() silently skips missing keys when averaging across folds. If fold 0 has "conv.weight" but fold 1 doesn't, the averaged model has conv.weight from fold 0 only (not averaged). This produces a corrupted model. Must warn or raise on key mismatch.

tdd-spec.red:
test_average_checkpoints_mismatched_keys_warns(): - Create 2 checkpoint state_dicts with different keys - Call _average_checkpoints([ckpt1, ckpt2]) - Assert WARNING logged about mismatched keys
test_average_checkpoints_empty_list_raises(): - Call _average_checkpoints([]) - Assert raises ValueError (not silent return per Rule 25)
test_average_checkpoints_single_ckpt_returns_copy(): - Call _average_checkpoints([ckpt1]) - Assert output equals input (identity for N=1)
tdd-spec.green: In _average_checkpoints(): 1. Empty list guard: raise ValueError("Cannot average 0 checkpoints") 2. Key mismatch detection: all_keys = [set(s.keys()) for s in state_dicts] if len(set.union(*all_keys)) != len(set.intersection(*all_keys)): missing = set.union(*all_keys) - set.intersection(*all_keys) logger.warning("Checkpoint key mismatch: %d keys not in all folds: %s", len(missing), list(missing)[:5])

phase.name: P1 Observability and Consistency
phase.description: Production observability and cross-file drift prevention

task.title: Add JSONL structured training event logging
task.description:
Training logs are unstructured text — sky jobs logs is slow, blocking, and not LLM-parseable. At 60+ jobs, manual log inspection is infeasible.
Add a structured JSONL event log written to the GCS-mounted checkpoint dir. Key events: epoch_start, epoch_end (with metrics), checkpoint_saved, training_complete. Also write a heartbeat.json file every 60s for quick status checks.
Claude Code can then `gsutil cat gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints/{job}/events.jsonl` instead of streaming unstructured logs.

tdd-spec.red:
test_event_logger_writes_jsonl(): - Create StructuredEventLogger(output_dir=tmp_path) - Call log_event("epoch_end", {"epoch": 5, "val_dice": 0.82, "vram_gb": 9.1}) - Read events.jsonl, parse each line as JSON - Assert event has timestamp, event_type, and payload keys
test_heartbeat_writes_json(): - Create StructuredEventLogger(output_dir=tmp_path) - Call update_heartbeat(epoch=5, loss=0.45, vram_gb=9.2) - Read heartbeat.json, parse as JSON - Assert it has epoch, loss, vram_gb, timestamp, status="training"
test_event_logger_is_noop_without_dir(): - Create StructuredEventLogger(output_dir=None) - Call log_event(...) — should not crash (graceful no-op)

task.title: Cross-file consistency: Docker image GAR path
task.description:
The GAR image path is duplicated in 9+ files with zero consistency tests. If any file drifts (e.g., during a merge conflict), SkyPilot pulls the wrong Docker image and all cloud jobs fail silently.
Source of truth: preflight_gcp.py:37 GAR_IMAGE constant. Must match: 5 SkyPilot YAMLs, gcp_spot.yaml.

tdd-spec.red:
Follow the pattern in test_hf_repo_consistency.py:
test_gar_image_source_constant_exists(): - Read preflight_gcp.py with AST, extract GAR_IMAGE constant - Assert it matches "europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest"
test_skypilot_yamls_use_correct_image(yaml_path): - Parametrize over all SkyPilot YAMLs with image_id - Parse YAML, extract image_id (strip "docker:" prefix) - Assert it matches GAR_IMAGE (strip "docker:" if present)
test_cloud_config_matches_gar_image(): - Read configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml - Extract docker_image key - Assert matches GAR_IMAGE
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — no implementation changes needed. Use yaml.safe_load for YAML, ast.parse for Python constants. Follow exact pattern from test_hf_repo_consistency.py.

task.title: Cross-file consistency: GCS bucket names
task.description: GCS bucket names are duplicated in 13+ files. The DVC data bucket and checkpoint bucket must be consistent across .dvc/config, SkyPilot YAMLs, preflight_gcp.py, and configs/dvc/remotes.yaml.

tdd-spec.red:
test_dvc_bucket_source_constant(): - Read preflight_gcp.py, extract GCS_BUCKET constant - Assert contains "minivess-mlops-dvc-data" (constant may include gs:// prefix) - Strip "gs://" prefix for cross-file comparisons
test_dvc_config_matches_preflight(dvc_config_path): - Read .dvc/config, extract GCS remote URL - Assert bucket name matches GCS_BUCKET
test_skypilot_dvc_pull_uses_correct_bucket(yaml_path): - Parametrize over SkyPilot YAMLs that have DVC pull in setup - Parse setup block for "dvc pull" commands - If bucket is referenced, assert it matches GCS_BUCKET
test_checkpoint_bucket_source_constant(): - Read preflight_gcp.py, extract CHECKPOINT_BUCKET - Assert "minivess-mlops-checkpoints"
test_skypilot_checkpoint_mount_matches(yaml_path): - Parametrize over SkyPilot YAMLs with file_mounts - Find GCS checkpoint mount - Assert bucket matches CHECKPOINT_BUCKET
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — no implementation changes needed. Parse YAML with yaml.safe_load, Python with ast.parse.

task.title: Remove POST_TRAINING_METHOD legacy fallback
task.description: train_flow.py:1657-1659 has a two-stage env var fallback: POST_TRAINING_METHODS → POST_TRAINING_METHOD (singular). Per Rule 26 (greenfield, no backward compat), the legacy singular form should be replaced with a hardcoded default "none,swag" so the safety net is preserved but the legacy var name is removed.

tdd-spec.red:
test_no_legacy_post_training_method_singular(): - Read train_flow.py source - Assert "POST_TRAINING_METHOD" (singular, without trailing S) does NOT appear as an os.environ.get key or argparse destination - Only "POST_TRAINING_METHODS" (plural) should exist
test_post_training_methods_env_var_parsed(): - Set POST_TRAINING_METHODS="none,swag" - Verify it's parsed as ["none", "swag"] in the training flow
tdd-spec.green: In train_flow.py: Remove the fallback line that checks POST_TRAINING_METHOD (singular). Keep only POST_TRAINING_METHODS (plural).

task.title: Add loss edge case tests for topology losses
task.description: Multiple topology losses have untested edge cases that produce silent zeros or NaN. Add parametrized tests for the top 3 risks identified by reviewers: - GraphTopologyLoss with all zero weights → silent zero loss - ccDice with unmatched components → NaN from 0/0 - BettiLoss with all-zero predictions → silent zero gradient

tdd-spec.red:
test_graph_topology_all_zero_weights_warns(): - GraphTopologyLoss(w_cbdice_cldice=0, w_skeleton_recall=0, w_cape=0) - Compute loss — assert it IS tensor(0.0) (correct) but ALSO verify a warning is logged about all-zero weights
test_ccdice_unmatched_components_finite(): - ccDice is a METRIC (topology_metrics.py:124 compute_ccdice), not a loss - Create pred with 3 connected components, GT with 0 overlap - Compute compute_ccdice(pred, target) - Assert result is finite (not NaN) — should be 0.0 - Guard the 0/0 division in IoU matrix (line 163)
test_betti_loss_all_zero_predictions_finite(): - logits = torch.full((1, 2, 8, 16, 16), -10.0) → softmax ≈ 0 foreground - Compute BettiLoss - Assert loss is finite and gradient exists
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying existing behavior is correct or exposing bugs. If bugs found, add guards in the loss implementations: - GraphTopologyLoss: warn if all weights are 0 - ccDice: clamp union to avoid 0/0 - BettiLoss: verify gradient flow on extreme inputs

verification.gate: uv run ruff check src/ tests/
